action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 18 failed with device mismatch in custom ray generation (CPU vs CUDA). The get_rays_for_view function created focal_length on CPU while directions on CUDA. Need to fix device handling and ensure get_ray_directions is called with correct positional arguments. Analysis: The custom rendering approach is promising to drastically reduce LPIPS (baseline 0.6179 from default views). We have the TripoSR source files locally (workspace/tsr/tsr/utils.py) so can match its logic. The error is trivial — tensors not on same device. Decision: Write a fixed test script that ensures all tensors on device, uses correct get_ray_directions signature (positional args: H, W, focal, principal, use_pixel_centers, normalize). Test on 1 validation object to measure LPIPS against ground truth. Simultaneously update predict.py with same fix. If test shows low LPIPS (<<0.62) then next iteration submit eval.
